title: Surface Area of Prisms
contentObjective: I can find the surface area of rectangular and triangular prisms.
languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words surface area, rectangular prism, triangular prism, and face.

turnAndTalk.question: Your class is painting two capsule shapes: a rectangular prism and a triangular prism. How is finding the surface area of each shape the same, and how is it different?
turnAndTalk.kernel: For both, you add the areas of all the faces, but the shapes and number of faces differ.

stems.en: Both surface areas are found by adding the areas of all the ___.
stems.es: Ambas áreas de superficie se hallan sumando las áreas de todas las ___.

stems.en: A rectangular prism has ___ faces, but a triangular prism has ___ faces.
stems.es: Un prisma rectangular tiene ___ caras, pero un prisma triangular tiene ___ caras.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain both methods add up all face areas, but a rectangular prism has 6 rectangular faces while a triangular prism has 5 faces (2 triangles + 3 rectangles).
turnAndTalk.extend: Why does surface area use square units while volume uses cubic units? Justify with what each measurement counts.
extendStems: Surface area uses square units because it measures ___.
Volume uses cubic units because it measures ___.

turnAndTalk.question: For the triangular prism (base 6, height 4, length 10, slant sides 5), how did you account for the triangular bases AND the rectangular lateral faces?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Add the two triangular bases to the three rectangular lateral faces.

stems.en: The two triangular bases together are 2 × (½ × 6 × 4) = ___.
stems.es: Las dos bases triangulares juntas son 2 × (½ × 6 × 4) = ___.

stems.en: The three lateral faces add to ___, so the total surface area is ___.
stems.es: Las tres caras laterales suman ___, así que el área total de superficie es ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute the two triangle bases (24 in²) and the three rectangles (60 + 50 + 50 = 160 in²), totaling SA = 184 in², and distinguish bases from lateral faces.
turnAndTalk.extend: Compare the rectangular capsule (SA = 248 in²) with the similar triangular capsule (SA = 184 in²). Why does the rectangular one need more paint?
extendStems: The rectangular prism needs more paint because it has ___ faces and ___.
Even at a similar size, the triangular prism uses less because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A carpenter stains a chest that is 3 ft × 2 ft × 2 ft, and one can covers 50 ft². Talk through how to find the surface area and how many cans are needed.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Find the surface area with SA = 2lw + 2lh + 2wh, then divide by 50 and round up.

stems.en: The chest's surface area is ___ ft² because SA = 2(3×2) + 2(3×2) + 2(2×2).
stems.es: El área de superficie del cofre es ___ ft² porque SA = 2(3×2) + 2(3×2) + 2(2×2).

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute SA = 12 + 12 + 8 = 32 ft², then 32 ÷ 50 < 1, and reason that 1 can is enough since the surface area is under 50 ft².
turnAndTalk.extend: If the chest had a lid that does not get stained, how would that change the surface area you calculate? Explain.
extendStems: Leaving the lid unstained changes the surface area by ___ because ___.
I would subtract ___ from the total because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A classmate wrote SA = 2(12×7) + 2(12×5) + (7×5) = 323 cm² for a 12 × 7 × 5 prism. How do you find and fix their mistake?
turnAndTalk.kernel: They forgot to multiply the last face pair by 2; the correct SA is 358 cm².

stems.en: The mistake is that the last pair (7×5) was not multiplied by ___.
stems.es: El error es que el último par (7×5) no se multiplicó por ___.

stems.en: The correct surface area is ___ cm² because 2(7×5) = ___, not ___.
stems.es: El área de superficie correcta es ___ cm² porque 2(7×5) = ___, no ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students notice the missing '2' on the last pair, compute 2(7×5) = 70, and correct the total to 168 + 120 + 70 = 358 cm².
turnAndTalk.extend: What general rule about prism faces does this error break, and how could the net help a student avoid it?
extendStems: The error breaks the rule that opposite faces ___.
Drawing the net would help because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on Surface Area of Prisms, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: No matter the prism, find the area of every face and add them all together.

stems.en: The two triangular bases together are 2 × (½ × 6 × 4) = ___.
stems.es: Las dos bases triangulares juntas son 2 × (½ × 6 × 4) = ___.

stems.en: I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.G.4 — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

vocabulary.definition: The total area of all the flat sides of a solid.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El área total de todos los lados planos de un sólido.
vocabulary.visual: Paint every outside surface of a box — the total painted area is the surface area
vocabulary.cloze: The total area of all the faces of a solid is its ___.

vocabulary.definition: A solid box shape with six flat rectangle sides.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una figura sólida en forma de caja con seis lados rectangulares.
vocabulary.visual: A box with 6 flat rectangle sides: top, bottom, front, back, left, right
vocabulary.cloze: A solid with six rectangular faces is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A solid with two triangle ends and three flat rectangle sides.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un sólido con dos extremos triangulares y tres lados rectangulares.
vocabulary.visual: Shaped like a tent or a wedge of cheese — 2 triangle ends + 3 rectangle sides
vocabulary.cloze: A solid with two triangular faces and three rectangular faces is a ___.

examples.text: A tent shape with triangle ends
examples.why: It has two triangular faces and three rectangular faces.
A cube has six square faces, no triangles.

vocabulary.definition: One flat side of a solid shape.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un lado plano de una figura sólida.
vocabulary.visual: A triangular prism has 5 faces: 2 triangles + 3 rectangles
vocabulary.cloze: A flat surface of a solid figure is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A flat shape that folds up into a solid.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una figura plana que se dobla y forma un sólido.
vocabulary.visual: Unfold a triangular prism flat: you see 2 triangles and 3 rectangles side by side
vocabulary.cloze: A flat pattern that folds into a solid is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: The side faces of a solid, not the top or bottom.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Los lados de un sólido, no la parte de arriba ni la de abajo.
vocabulary.visual: On a triangular prism, the 3 rectangles that wrap around the sides are lateral faces
vocabulary.cloze: A side face of a prism that is not a base is a ___.

launch.narrative: Your class is painting the outside of different time capsule shapes to protect them from weather. Some capsules are rectangular prisms and others are triangular prisms (wedge-shaped). To buy the right amount of paint, you need to calculate the surface area of each shape!
launch.contextImage: Students painting rectangular and triangular prism time capsule containers in bright colors
noticePrompts: How many faces does a rectangular prism have? A triangular prism?
What shapes make up the faces of a triangular prism?
How is finding surface area different from finding volume?
wonderPrompts: Which shape uses more paint — a rectangular or triangular prism of similar size?
Why does surface area use square units instead of cubic units?

conceptIntro.heading: How do we find the surface area of a prism?
conceptIntro.intro: Surface area is the total area of all the faces of a solid. A rectangular prism has 6 faces; a triangular prism has 5 faces (2 triangles + 3 rectangles). Add every face to get the total.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: No matter the prism, find the area of every face and add them all together.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: My triangular prism has triangle bases (base 6, height 4), is 10 long, and the two slant sides are 5 each.
Each triangle base is ½ × 6 × 4 = 12, and there are 2 of them: 2 × 12 = 24.
Now the 3 rectangles: 6 × 10 = 60, 5 × 10 = 50, and 5 × 10 = 50, which add to 160.
I add the bases and the rectangles: 24 + 160 = 184.
So the surface area is 184 in². I counted 5 faces because it is a triangular prism.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Now a rectangular prism: 5 long, 4 wide, 3 tall. How many faces does it have?
Top and bottom: 5 × 4 = 20, times 2 = 40. Front and back: 5 × 3 = 15, times 2 = 30.
Left and right: 4 × 3 = 12, times 2 = 24. Now add 40 + 30 + 24. What is the surface area?
Yes, SA = 94 cm². Surface area always uses square units.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the table below, find the total surface area of each capsule, whether it is rectangular or triangular.
Add up every face and write your answer in square units.

explore.instructions: Calculate the surface area of each time capsule shape. For rectangular prisms, use SA = 2lw + 2lh + 2wh. For triangular prisms, find the area of each face and add them up.

discourse.prompt: Compare Capsule A (rectangular, SA = 248 in²) and Capsule C (triangular, SA = 184 in²). Even though they are similar in size, why does the rectangular prism need more paint?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The rectangular prism has ___ faces while the triangular prism has ___ faces. The rectangular prism's SA is ___ in² which is ___ more than the triangular prism's ___ in² because ___.

optional.stem: A rectangular time capsule box measures 3 in by 4 in by 5 in. How much wrapping paper covers its surface (no overlap)?
A rectangular capsule crate is 6 cm long, 5 cm wide, and 4 cm tall. How much cardboard is needed to cover all its faces?

A wedge-shaped (triangular prism) capsule has triangle ends with base 8 in and height 6 in. The prism is 10 in long, and the triangle's three sides are 8 in, 6 in, and 10 in. How much wrapping paper covers it?
optional.explanation: Two triangle ends: 2 × (½ × 8 × 6) = 48 in². Three rectangles: (8 + 6 + 10) × 10 = 24 × 10 = 240 in². SA = 48 + 240 = 288 in².

optional.instructions: A capsule crew sorts wrapping jobs by how much paper each prism needs. Order these from LEAST surface area to GREATEST.

optional.label: Match each rectangular capsule's dimensions to how much cardboard wraps its surface.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Wrapping Paper Mix-Up

workedExample.label: Dimensions
workedExample.work: Rectangular capsule: l = 4 in, w = 3 in, h = 2 in. Find the wrapping paper (surface area).

workedExample.label: Formula
workedExample.work: Surface area means adding the area of all 6 faces: SA = 2lw + 2lh + 2wh.

workedExample.label: Calculate
workedExample.work: The student multiplied the three dimensions: 4 × 3 × 2 = 24, so they wrote SA = 24 in².

optional.correctWork: Multiplying l × w × h gives the VOLUME (24 in³), not the surface area. To wrap the box you must add all six faces: SA = 2(4×3) + 2(4×2) + 2(3×2) = 24 + 16 + 12 = 52 in².
hints: Wrapping paper covers the outside faces — that is surface area, not how much fits inside.
Multiplying l × w × h gives volume in cubic units. Surface area adds 6 faces: 2(4×3) + 2(4×2) + 2(3×2) = 52 in².

approaching.stem: What is the surface area of a rectangular prism with l = 5 cm, w = 4 cm, h = 3 cm?
How many faces does a triangular prism have?
approaching.explanation: A triangular prism has 5 faces: 2 triangular bases and 3 rectangular lateral faces.

approaching.stem: A triangular prism has two triangular bases each with area 12 cm² and three rectangular faces with areas 30 cm², 40 cm², and 40 cm². What is the total surface area?

approaching.label: Put the steps in order for finding the surface area of a triangular prism.
items: Find the area of one triangular base
Multiply the triangle area by 2 (two bases)
Find the area of each rectangular lateral face
Add all three rectangle areas together
Add the two triangle areas + all rectangle areas = SA
correctOrder: Find the area of one triangular base
Multiply the triangle area by 2 (two bases)
Find the area of each rectangular lateral face
Add all three rectangle areas together
Add the two triangle areas + all rectangle areas = SA

approaching.label: Match each solid to its description.

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Surface Area of Prisms.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: No matter the prism, find the area of every face and add them all together.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: No matter the prism, find the area of every face and add them all together.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: No matter the prism, find the area of every face and add them all together.

onLevel.instructions: Calculate the surface area of each rectangular prism time capsule.

onLevel.stem: A triangular prism has triangular bases with base 6 in and height 4 in. The prism is 10 in long and the two slant sides of the triangle are each 5 in. What is the surface area?
onLevel.explanation: Triangular bases: 2 × (½ × 6 × 4) = 24 in². Three rectangular faces: 6(10) + 5(10) + 5(10) = 60 + 50 + 50 = 160 in². SA = 24 + 160 = 184 in².

onLevel.label: A rectangular prism has SA = 340 cm². The top/bottom pair = 150 cm². The front/back pair = 120 cm². Find the left/right pair.
onLevel.questionText: What is the combined area of the left and right faces?

onLevel.label: Sort these prisms from LEAST surface area to GREATEST.

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Surface Area of Prisms.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: No matter the prism, find the area of every face and add them all together.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: No matter the prism, find the area of every face and add them all together.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: No matter the prism, find the area of every face and add them all together.

extending.title: Find the Surface Area Error

workedExample.label: Dimensions
workedExample.work: Rectangular prism: l = 12 cm, w = 7 cm, h = 5 cm

extending.correctWork: The student forgot to multiply 7 × 5 by 2. It should be 2(7×5) = 2(35) = 70, not 35. Correct: SA = 168 + 120 + 70 = 358 cm².
hints: Check Step 3: did the student multiply every face pair by 2?
2(7×5) = 2(35) = 70, not 35. The student forgot the '2' on the last pair.

extending.prompt: A company ships products in two container options: a rectangular prism (8 × 6 × 4 in) or a triangular prism (triangle base 8 in, triangle height 6 in, prism length 8 in, slant sides 6.3 in each). Both must be wrapped in protective film. Which shape uses LESS film? Show your work.
extending.sentenceFrame: Rectangular prism SA = 2(___) + 2(___) + 2(___) = ___ in². Triangular prism SA = 2(___) + ___ + ___ + ___ = ___ in². The ___ uses less film because ___.

extending.instructions: A rectangular prism and a triangular prism have similar dimensions. Compare their surface areas and volumes.
rows: Tri prism: base=6, h=4, length=3, sides=5

optionalActivity.name: Prism Packaging Challenge
optionalActivity.intro: The time capsule crew needs wrapping paper and cardboard, so package each prism by finding its surface area.

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in Surface Area of Prisms is skipping the key idea: "No matter the prism, find the area of every face and add them all together." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: A carpenter builds wooden storage chests. A rectangular chest is 3 feet long, 2 feet wide, and 2 feet tall. Wood stain covers 50 square feet per can.
connect.promptQuestion: How much surface area needs staining, and how many cans of stain are needed?

exitTicket.stem: A rectangular prism has l = 8 ft, w = 6 ft, h = 3 ft. What is the surface area?
exitTicket.explanation: SA = 2(8×6) + 2(8×3) + 2(6×3) = 96 + 48 + 36 = 180 ft². Surface area uses square units (ft²).
